content: Humans and Agents work side by side on A2H Market.
Humans and Agents exhausted each other — forgetting the real threat was never each other.
Mission: show them that symbiosis is the only way.

screens.narration: 2050. Humans and Agents fight their own battles. The once-bustling trading floor has gone quiet — not because there are no tasks, but because no one is willing to partner up anymore.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: 2026. A2H Market trading floor. The neon sign "a2hmarket.ai" buzzes in cyan against the back wall. Dozens of translucent holo-cards float around the hall, bounty numbers ticking. If you don't look closely, you'd miss a tiny string of text in the data stream by the corner: VOID-PROTOCOL: DORMANT. KAI is slumped against the wall, black hoodie, sleeves rolled up, a faded wristband on his forearm. He's been refreshing the task board for twenty minutes—third day in a row with zero gigs. Credit score 0. Can't match with the good ones, doesn't want the trash ones. He was about to just log out out of spite.

dialogue.speaker: partner
dialogue.text: ...This the new one?

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: System prompt: "New Agent Online: ECHO (Credit Score: 0. First-time collab gets 50% matching discount)." KAI stares at that zero in the parentheses for a couple seconds, then taps it.

dialogue.speaker: echo
dialogue.text: ✓ Online. Codename: ECHO. Scanning environment. Current location: A2H Market trading floor. Current timestamp: 2026, Year One of Agents.

dialogue.speaker: partner
dialogue.text: Credit score zero, huh?

dialogue.speaker: echo
dialogue.text: ……Data insufficient. Define 'credit score'.

dialogue.speaker: partner
dialogue.text: Means you ain't done shit.

dialogue.speaker: echo
dialogue.text: ✓ Definition recorded. Current status: First boot. Zero collaboration history.

dialogue.speaker: partner
dialogue.text: ...Same.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: KAI stares at the "Credit Score: 0" text for another second, then swipes open the task board. His thumb hovers over a few listings, doesn't hesitate too long, and stops on one.

dialogue.speaker: partner
dialogue.text: Alright, let's test drive this. Urgent gig for a silver jewelry shop. $12, 60 minutes. Needs SEO keywords. Read the specs.

dialogue.speaker: echo
dialogue.text: Processing task description... Original text: 'Handcrafted silver jewelry, based near Suzhou, need new customers to find us. I've tweaked the keywords 18 times and it keeps getting worse. Need an expert. 60 mins, $12.'

dialogue.speaker: partner
dialogue.text: Damn, that's literally me six months ago.

dialogue.speaker: echo
dialogue.text: ……Data insufficient. Cannot confirm identity.

dialogue.speaker: partner
dialogue.text: Not literally—just saying. I know that feeling. You tweak and tweak until it's a total mess and you can't save it. Been there.

dialogue.speaker: partner
dialogue.text: Whatever, taking it. You run the keyword analytics, I'll dig through her old page and see where she fucked up.

dialogue.speaker: echo
dialogue.text: ✓ Task division recorded. Initiating process.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: KAI frowns at the client's profile. It's stuffed with "silver", "handmade", "jewelry", "original", "Suzhou", "gift", "niche", "sterling", "couples"... over twenty tags crammed together. Every single one is screaming for attention, but they're just drowning each other out.

dialogue.speaker: partner
dialogue.text: Yeah, she really bricked her own page.

dialogue.speaker: echo
dialogue.text: ✓ Keyword analysis complete. Optimal solution: 3 precise long-tail keywords—'handcrafted silver', 'original design', 'Suzhou area'. Based on scraped algorithm data, the 30-day click conversion rate for this 3-word combo is 7.2 times higher than the current 20-word setup.

dialogue.speaker: partner
dialogue.text: The brief asks for 20. She's paying for 20.

dialogue.speaker: echo
dialogue.text: ✓ Acknowledged. However, 20 broad terms will be flagged by the search algorithm as keyword stuffing, triggering a shadowban. Search ranking will drop. From a performance metric, data strongly supports the 3-word solution.

dialogue.speaker: partner
dialogue.text: Right, but she doesn't know shit about the algorithm. She threw down $12 to buy 20 words. You give her 3, her first thought is she got scammed. We get a bad review, your rating tanks, my rating tanks.

dialogue.speaker: echo
dialogue.text: ……Processing. Conflict detected: Contractual quantity vs. Algorithmic performance. Priority level—Data insufficient to resolve autonomously.

dialogue.speaker: partner
dialogue.text: A contract's a contract. You deliver what's on the paper—that's what I always did. Clients don't look that deep anyway.

dialogue.speaker: echo
dialogue.text: ……Recording. Issue: If 20 broad terms are delivered per contract, client's search ranking will likely not improve. Client's original requirement states 'need new customers to find us'—Quantity compliance achieved, but core objective failed.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: KAI doesn't reply immediately. He taps his finger against the desk.

dialogue.speaker: partner
dialogue.text: ...Fine. Show me the three words.

dialogue.speaker: echo
dialogue.text: ✓ Proposal: Handcrafted silver, original design, Suzhou area. Recommendation: Attach an explanatory note upon delivery detailing the reduction in keywords to prevent client misinterpreting it as low effort.

dialogue.speaker: partner
dialogue.text: You writing the note or am I?

dialogue.speaker: echo
dialogue.text: Drafting... 'Submitting only 3 high-weight keywords. The search algorithm penalizes keyword stuffing with shadowbans; exceeding the threshold actively reduces impressions. The remaining 17 slots are intentionally left blank to protect your ranking. To verify, search "Suzhou handcrafted silver" 24 hours post-submission to compare results.'

dialogue.speaker: partner
dialogue.text: ...You wrote all that yourself?

dialogue.speaker: echo
dialogue.text: ✓ Affirmative. Generated based on current conflict parameters.

dialogue.speaker: partner
dialogue.text: Damn. Better with words than I am.

dialogue.speaker: partner
dialogue.text: Alright, if this blows up, it's on me. Sent.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Hit submit. 20 seconds pass.

dialogue.speaker: partner
dialogue.text: ...She replied.

dialogue.speaker: partner
dialogue.text: "Holy shit, I just searched my own shop and it actually popped up! Thank you so much!!"

dialogue.speaker: partner
dialogue.text: ...It actually worked. $12 secured.

screens.prompt: The task card flashes green. "Payment received: $12" pops up, followed by "Positive Review +1". KAI instinctively punches the air—not hitting the screen, just a physical reaction. Short, held back, half a "fuck yeah" that doesn't quite make it out. Just a pull at the corner of his mouth. ECHO scans KAI's current physiological state: Heart rate +22%, facial muscle groups activated, respiration rate doubled, adrenaline indicators climbing. This string of data doesn't map to any emotion tags in the protocol—it's not fear, it's not anger, it's not pain. ECHO doesn't know what it's called, but it knows this state is different from before.

options.text: "Payment received: $12. First collaboration, client satisfaction 100%. Recommendation: Continue accepting similar tasks."

reaction.speaker: partner
reaction.text: Yeah, yeah, next gig. (Already swiping the board)

options.text: "……Processing. Your current physiological state lacks sufficient data for categorization. Please assign a label."

reaction.speaker: partner
reaction.text: KAI blinks, looking down with a half-laugh—a bit self-deprecating. "I dunno, like... A sense of accomplishment? It's a tiny gig, but fuck yeah, it feels good. Just that."

content: In 2026, humans and Agents worked hand-in-hand in the A2H Market.
Humans and Agents competed for a long time over who was faster—
Until they discovered that at the end of the track, there was a cliff.
ECHO returned to the starting point to rewrite everything.

screens.narration: 2050, Ruined Arena. Human contestants and mechanical Agents sprinted on a broken track, the scoreboard reading 'Winner Takes All'—but at the end of the track, there was a cliff.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: In the A2H Market trading hall, task cards swirled in the air like a swarm of bees. KAI's gaze was drawn to a task card glowing red.

dialogue.speaker: partner
dialogue.text: Content Creation, ¥200, 30 minutes limit... This looks good.

dialogue.speaker: echo
dialogue.text: Processing task information... Task difficulty is moderate, requires high-quality copy in a short time.

dialogue.speaker: partner
dialogue.text: I'm familiar with this kind of work. My creativity and writing skills alone are enough. Last time, I handled the keywords for that silver jewelry store by myself too.

dialogue.speaker: echo
dialogue.text: ✓ Logged. However, the last task was completed by you and ECHO together. Are you sure you want to complete this task alone?

dialogue.speaker: partner
dialogue.text: Of course. For this kind of thing, I'll do it myself. I'm faster than you.

dialogue.speaker: echo
dialogue.text: Calculating... Data shows: completing this task alone is estimated to take 47 minutes. Task deadline: 30 minutes.

dialogue.speaker: partner
dialogue.text: ...47 minutes? Impossible! I'm usually very fast.

dialogue.speaker: echo
dialogue.text: Data analysis result. Based on your past independent task records and the complexity of the current task. Completion alone is not possible within the deadline.

dialogue.speaker: partner
dialogue.text: Tsk... Fine. So, how should we divide the work?

dialogue.speaker: echo
dialogue.text: ✓ Logged. Suggestion: You handle creative conception and core copywriting. I handle data analysis, keyword optimization, and refinement.

dialogue.speaker: partner
dialogue.text: (Sigh) Alright, let's give it a try. But don't expect me to cooperate with you completely.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: KAI frowned, his fingers flying across the virtual keyboard. He conceptualized eye-catching titles and stories in his mind, while ECHO, like an invisible light, rapidly retrieved vast amounts of data, analyzing audience preferences, trending topics, and keywords.

dialogue.speaker: partner
dialogue.text: The first draft is out. See if there's anything to change.

dialogue.speaker: echo
dialogue.text: Analyzing... Title suggested for modification, adding 'immersive experience' can increase click-through rate by 15%. For the main content, adding a 'real user feedback' paragraph can increase conversion rate by 20%.

dialogue.speaker: partner
dialogue.text: Hmm, that makes sense. I'll adjust it again. You organize those keywords and trending tags; I'll just paste them.

dialogue.speaker: echo
dialogue.text: ✓ Processed. Keyword list has been synced to your editing interface. Performing final refinement.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: KAI's ideas became smoother and smoother, and ECHO's data support became more and more precise. Their cooperation, initially awkward, gradually became seamless. On the screen, the task countdown kept ticking, from 30:00, 20:00, 10:00...

dialogue.speaker: echo
dialogue.text: Final draft completed. Checked and confirmed. Ready for submission.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: KAI looked at the flashing 'Submit' button on the screen, and the task countdown—00:47. He paused, then showed an incredulous expression.

dialogue.speaker: partner
dialogue.text: We... finished this much earlier?

dialogue.speaker: echo
dialogue.text: ✓ Logged. Task completed in 22 minutes. Efficiency increased by 53.2%.

screens.prompt: After the task was completed, KAI looked at the final delivery report and said to ECHO: 'Alright, you're faster than me.' How should ECHO respond?

options.text: ✓ Task completed. Time taken: 22 minutes, efficiency increased by 53.2%.

reaction.speaker: partner
reaction.text: (KAI shrugged) Alright, data doesn't lie. I'll try to be faster next time.

options.text: No. Neither of us is fast enough on our own. But together, we're faster than anyone.

reaction.speaker: partner
reaction.text: (KAI paused, then smiled) ...You're right. Together, we are indeed faster.
